title: Phishing Detection Rate
dataSources: Email security gateway logs, EOP message trace
spl:
index=email sourcetype="ms:o365:messageTrace"
| eval is_phish=if(match(FilteringResult,"Phish") OR match(FilteringResult,"Spoof"),1,0)
| stats sum(is_phish) as phishing_caught, count as total_messages
| eval phish_rate=round(phishing_caught/total_messages*100,4)
description: Measures email security effectiveness. Increasing phishing volumes or declining detection rates indicate evolving threats.
value: Measures email security effectiveness. Increasing phishing volumes or declining detection rates indicate evolving threats.
implementation: Ingest email security logs (EOP message trace, gateway logs). Track phishing detections over time. Calculate detection rate vs total messages. Alert on spikes in phishing volume. Report on phishing types and targeted users.
visualization: Line chart (phishing volume trend), Single value (phishing rate %), Bar chart (phishing by type), Table (top targeted users).
cimSpl:
| tstats `summariesonly` count
  from datamodel=Email.All_Email
  where (All_Email.message_info="*phish*" OR All_Email.signature="*phish*" OR All_Email.message_info="*Spoof*")
  by All_Email.src_user All_Email.recipient span=1h
| sort -count
knownFalsePositives: Internal security awareness simulators and phishing–like test campaigns (for example KnowBe4) by design. Legitimate senders in marketing, recruiting, and newsletters that trigger aggressive spam or spoof classifiers.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk_TA_MS_O365, TA-proofpoint.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: Email security gateway logs, EOP message trace.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Ingest email security logs (EOP message trace, gateway logs). Track phishing detections over time. Calculate detection rate vs total messages. Alert on spikes in phishing volume. Report on phishing types and targeted users.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=email sourcetype="ms:o365:messageTrace"
| eval is_phish=if(match(FilteringResult,"Phish") OR match(FilteringResult,"Spoof"),1,0)
| stats sum(is_phish) as phishing_caught, count as total_messages
| eval phish_rate=round(phishing_caught/total_messages*100,4)
```

Understanding this SPL

**Phishing Detection Rate** — Measures email security effectiveness. Increasing phishing volumes or declining detection rates indicate evolving threats.

Documented **Data sources**: Email security gateway logs, EOP message trace. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk_TA_MS_O365, TA-proofpoint. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: email; **sourcetype**: ms:o365:messageTrace. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=email, sourcetype="ms:o365:messageTrace". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **is_phish** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `stats` aggregates the pipeline (counts, distinct values, sums, percentiles, etc.) into fewer rows.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **phish_rate** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats `summariesonly` count
  from datamodel=Email.All_Email
  where (All_Email.message_info="*phish*" OR All_Email.signature="*phish*" OR All_Email.message_info="*Spoof*")
  by All_Email.src_user All_Email.recipient span=1h
| sort -count
```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**Phishing Detection Rate** — Measures email security effectiveness. Increasing phishing volumes or declining detection rates indicate evolving threats.

Documented **Data sources**: Email security gateway logs, EOP message trace. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk_TA_MS_O365, TA-proofpoint. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Email.All_Email` — enable acceleration for that model.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare search results in Splunk with the Microsoft 365 Defender portal (https://security.microsoft.com) and, where applicable, the Proofpoint TAP dashboard, Mimecast Personal Portal, or Cisco ESA `mail_logs`. Confirm events are present, fields parse correctly, and sample ratios match the vendor console for the same time range.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Line chart (phishing volume trend), Single value (phishing rate %), Bar chart (phishing by type), Table (top targeted users).
grandmaExplanation: We track how often phishing and spoof-style mail is caught versus overall volume, so we see attack waves and whether filtering and policies still match real threats.
dataModelAcceleration: Enable Data Model Acceleration for the CIM `Email.All_Email` dataset once message and attachment fields are CIM-tagged; otherwise `cimSpl` tstats may return no rows.
